findings.location: s1 — 複数の Form に対するバリデーション
findings.description: The HTML form code example from the source is not included in the knowledge file. The source provides a concrete `<form method="POST">` block showing name attributes like `form.user.name`, `form.user.remarks`, `form.user.id`, `form.address.zipCode`, `form.address.address1`, etc. While the 3-level naming convention is explained conceptually in s1, the actual HTML code example demonstrates the correct pattern for both user fields and address fields together, which is a concrete usage pattern an AI would need to reproduce correct HTML.
findings.source_evidence: Source section '複数の Form に対するバリデーション' contains a `.. code-block:: html` block showing `<form method="POST">` with inputs named `form.user.name`, `form.user.remarks`, `form.user.id`, `form.address.zipCode`, `form.address.address1`, `form.address.address2`, `form.address.building`.

findings.description: `ApplicationException` is a PascalCase class used in the s1 code example (`throw new ApplicationException(result.getMessages())`) but is absent from the s1 hints array.
findings.source_evidence: Source code block: `throw new ApplicationException(result.getMessages());` in the validateAndConvertRequest usage example.

findings.description: `ApplicationException` is a PascalCase class used in the s2 Action implementation code example but is absent from the s2 hints array.
